title: Information scrambling in all-to-all interacting models
authors: Abhik Kumar Saha, Tanay Pathak, Masaki Tezuka
abstract: Information scrambling is a hallmark of quantum chaos and thermalization in isolated quantum many-body systems. We investigate scrambling dynamics in the all-to-all interacting spin Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev (SYK)-$q$ model using both pure- and mixed-state entanglement measures. We show that von-Neumann and Rényi entropies exhibit rapid growth followed by saturation near Haar-random values, signaling efficient scrambling. The scrambling rate reveals a nontrivial dependence on the interaction order, system size, and Hamiltonian scaling. We further employ mixed-state entanglement as a powerful probe of information scrambling. We numerically find a universal relation between the Rényi-1/2 mutual information and entanglement negativity for minimal interaction order in the early growth regime. Furthermore, entanglement negativity displays a Page-curve-like behavior under unequal subsystem partitioning, characterized by the birth, spread, and eventual death of quantum correlations. Our results provide a generic description of information scrambling using entanglement dynamics in all-to-all interacting spin systems with multi-body interactions.
summary: This work analyzes information scrambling in the all-to-all interacting spin SYK-q model using advanced entanglement measures. It demonstrates that entanglement grows rapidly towards a random limit, confirming efficient quantum chaos. The research provides a detailed comparison of how different interaction orders and initial states affect this scrambling process.
main_problem: The paper investigates information scrambling and thermalization dynamics in highly interacting, all-to-all coupled quantum many-body systems.
main_result: Entanglement measures show rapid growth followed by saturation near Haar-random values, confirming efficient scrambling. The scrambling rate exhibits a non-trivial dependence on the interaction order and system size.
method: The study employs numerical simulations to calculate various entanglement measures (von-Neumann, Rényi entropies, entanglement negativity) for the spin Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev (SYK)-q model.
model_or_system: The primary model is the all-to-all interacting spin Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev (SYK)-q model, characterized by a Hamiltonian involving multi-body spin interactions. The analysis compares dynamics across different interaction orders ($q$) and initial quantum states.
key_observables: Von-Neumann and Rényi entropies, entanglement negativity, mutual information, and the scrambling rate.
important_parameters: Interaction order ($q$), system size ($L$), and the choice of initial quantum state (e.g., random, superposition, Néel state).
main_assumptions: The analysis often compares results to the theoretical Haar-random limit and sometimes restricts calculations to specific parity sectors.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the time evolution of entanglement measures for various $q$ and initial states, showing initial growth and eventual saturation towards random values. Comparisons are made between different entanglement measures and time scales.
paper_structure: The paper systematically analyzes entanglement dynamics by first establishing the general behavior in the SYK-q model, then comparing different entanglement measures (pure vs. mixed state), and finally investigating the dependence of scrambling on the initial quantum state and the interaction order $q$.
why_interesting: The study provides a generic framework for understanding how information spreads in complex, highly interacting quantum systems, drawing parallels between quantum chaos, thermalization, and concepts from quantum gravity (like black hole scrambling).
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Evolution of (a) the average von-Neumann EE and (b) the 2-RE with time for spin-SYK model with q = 2, 3, 4, 5 in natural timescale. (c) and (d) shows the same in energy fixed timescale. The initial state is given by Eq. (3). We consider L = 17, even parity block for even q and L = 16 with full Hilbert space for odd q. The sub-systems sizes are LA = LB = 8 for both the cases. The horizontal black dot–dashed line corresponds to the Haar random values. We consider 220−L Hamiltonian realizations and initial state is changed over each realization.

FIG. 2. The dynamics of the average von-Neumann EE for different values of q and (a) L = 6 (even q) and L = 5 (odd q), (b) L = 8 (even q) and L = 7 (odd q), (c) L = 10 (even q) and L = 9 (odd q), and (d) L = 12 (even q) and L = 11 (odd q), for the spin SYK-q model with the prefactor set to unity. We consider 220−L Hamiltonian realizations. The horizontal black dot-dashed lines indicate the Haar random values. The inset shows the zoomed view of the EE in the early time regime for q = 4 and q = 5.
